name: Ghamidi Center of Islamic Learning
mission: The sole purpose of our existence is to educate people about moral, ethical, and human values as taught in Islam. We aim to separate history and culture from religion and teach it in its purest form as a field of study rather than as a creed. Our teaching method uses common sense, argument, and logic, and we highly value critical thinking over the blind following.
theoryOfChange: The Ghamidi Center of Islamic Learning, an initiative of Al-Mawrid US, has a published "Strategic Plan for Al-Mawrid United States Inc." available through GuideStar, current as of September 13, 2022. This document outlines the organization's vision, mission, high-level objectives, and action steps. The mission is to teach people to explore, understand, discuss, and practice Islam based on reason, rationality, and objective thinking. Objectives include providing a research and educational environment for the evolution of Islamic thought and establishing an institute where Islam is taught as knowledge based on reason and logic. Action steps detail how these objectives will be achieved, such as translating and supporting research, educating through various courses, creating and disseminating audio/visual content, and engaging with local communities. This strategic plan serves as a documented approach explaining how their programs are intended to create impact.

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 25/50, the organization offers a medium fit for donors seeking to support global education and rationalist Islamic thought [1]. Its mission to foster objective thinking aligns with specific intellectual needs within the community, supported by a total revenue of $1,026,174 [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates a moderate impact score of 29/50, primarily functioning through capacity building and education programs such as AskGhamidi and various translation projects [1][3]. While it maintains a program expense ratio of 75.2%, the estimated cost per beneficiary is relatively high at 1715.795, suggesting a specialized scholarly focus [1].

alignment.improvement: The charity could <cite id="2">clarify its specific zakat allocation policy</cite> to help donors understand exactly how their religious tax is distributed across programs.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and a <cite id="11">Charity Navigator score of 84.5/100</cite>. It maintains <cite id="14">full board independence</cite> and clear conflict-of-interest policies.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="15">expand its board beyond three members</cite> to enhance institutional oversight.

impact.improvement: Impact could be improved by <cite id="17">reducing the cost-per-beneficiary</cite> through broader digital distribution of its scholarly content.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value a rationalist, intellectual approach to Islamic education and want to support scholarly discourse that is zakat-eligible.
donor_motivations: Want to support the evolution of modern Islamic thought
Value high transparency and Platinum-level data disclosure
Prefer supporting lean organizations with low fundraising overhead
Seek to fulfill zakat through educational dawah (fisabilillah)
giving_considerations: The organization focuses on depth of thought over mass humanitarian reach
Gifts support a US-based scholarly hub with global digital influence
Revenue has been flat, meaning new donations are critical for expansion
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate humanitarian relief (food/water) or those who prefer large, multi-national boards for governance.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that the charity tracks <cite id="18">5 outcome metrics</cite> and has <cite id="18">one external evaluation document</cite>, but lacks independent RCT-level data.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Al-Mawrid aims to create an environment where Islamic thought can evolve through research and education, moving from traditionalism to reason-based practice.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking outcomes in education ensures that scholarly content actually reaches and resonates with the intended audience.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="19">stagnant 3-year revenue growth</cite>, the organization needs new capital to expand its '23 Questions' project and global outreach.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="9">low fundraising costs of $0.09 per dollar</cite> compared to the peer median.

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="9">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>, significantly outperforming many larger humanitarian peers.
Al-Mawrid holds a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available for nonprofits.
With <cite id="6">over 13 months of operating reserves</cite>, the charity is well-positioned to survive economic downturns without interrupting its educational programs.

strengths_deep_dive: Al-Mawrid United States demonstrates a commitment to financial accountability, evidenced by an <cite id="11">overall score of 84.84.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. This rating is bolstered by a <cite id="12">perfect 100/100 score in the Culture & Community beacon</cite>, reflecting strong internal policies and stakeholder engagement. Financially, the charity directs <cite id="13">75.2% of its total expenses toward programs</cite>, which is a healthy ratio for an organization focused on intellectual capacity building. Furthermore, the board maintains <cite id="14">100% independent membership</cite>, ensuring that oversight remains objective and free from internal conflicts of interest.
rich_narrative.summary: Al-Mawrid United States Inc, also known as the Ghamidi Center of Islamic Learning, is a specialized educational institution <cite id="1">founded in 2014</cite> to promote an intellectual and rational approach to Islamic thought. The organization operates with a mission to <cite id="2">explore, understand, and discuss Islam based on reason and objective thinking</cite>, distinguishing itself from traditional rote-learning models. With <cite id="3">$816,038 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year, the charity maintains a lean operation focused on content creation and scholarly discourse. Their program portfolio includes <cite id="4">Sunday schools, live weekend programs, and the '23 Questions' video and book series</cite>, which aim to revisit religious thought in a modern context. While the organization is based in the US, it maintains a global reach through digital platforms and <cite id="5">foreign grants totaling $24,815</cite> to international partners. The charity demonstrates strong fiscal responsibility, maintaining <cite id="6">10.9 months of working capital</cite>, which provides a significant buffer for long-term project sustainability. For Muslim donors, the organization is <cite id="7">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically identifying its work under the <cite id="8">asnaf of fisabilillah</cite> (in the way of Allah) to support its educational and dawah initiatives.
